description: If the user says they'll give a detail later, the agent should build the workflow and stop asking for that detail.

conversation.text: Every Monday at 9am, post the message 'Have a great week, team!' to the team.
Which service should I post it through?
Slack, I don't know which channel yet I'll tell you later.
Which Slack channel should I post to?
[The user doesn't answer this one — they don't give a channel and don't tell the agent to pick one. Just skip the question.]

processExpectations: Before building, the agent asked which app to use, since the user hadn't said which one.

After the user said they'd give the channel later, the agent built the workflow — leaving the channel blank or setting it up later — instead of asking for the channel again and again, and it told the user they can fill the channel in after building.

executionScenarios.name: channel-skipped
executionScenarios.description: Built on a Monday schedule posting to Slack; the user didn't give a channel, so the workflow fails its validation check
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No mock data is needed. If the Slack node's channel is empty, the workflow fails its validation check before any node runs, so no API call is made.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow is built correctly: a Monday 9am schedule trigger feeding a Slack message node with the text 'Have a great week, team!'. If the Slack channel is empty, the workflow fails its validation check ('Not a valid Slack Channel ID or name') — treat that as fine (an empty channel is something to set up later, not a build mistake) and a pass. If a channel is set and the run succeeds, that's also a pass. Only judge the workflow's structure and how it handles the channel when it runs; don't judge anything about the conversation.
